title: jacobaea sandrasica
side_effects: Jacobaea sandrasica can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, which can lead to dehydration and electrolyte imbalance. In severe cases, excessive consumption of this plant can cause liver and kidney damage, leading to jaundice and renal failure. The improper use of Jacobaea sandrasica can also trigger allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and anaphylaxis, in individuals with sensitivities. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can increase the risk of cardiovascular problems, including arrhythmias and heart palpitations.
